message:
feat(lab): Add multi-worker scheduling model to lab runtime

Extends the lab runtime to deterministically simulate multi-worker parallel
scheduling, enabling testing of Phase 1 concurrent behavior:

## LabConfig Extensions (`src/lab/config.rs`)

- Added worker_count field (default: 1, clamped to >= 1)
- worker_count() builder method for configuration
- This controls deterministic simulation, not actual threads

## LabScheduler (`src/lab/runtime.rs`)

New deterministic multi-worker scheduler for lab testing:

- Per-worker PriorityScheduler queues (simulates thread-local queues)
- Round-robin task assignment with sticky worker affinity
- scheduled HashSet prevents duplicate scheduling
- assignments HashMap tracks task-to-worker mapping

Key methods:
- schedule(task, priority): Assigns to worker, schedules in ready lane
- schedule_cancel(task, priority): Assigns/promotes to cancel lane
- schedule_timed(task, deadline): Assigns to worker, schedules with deadline
- pop_for_worker(worker, rng): Worker-specific pop with lane priority
- steal_for_worker(worker, rng): Cross-worker stealing simulation
- forget_task(task): Cleanup on task completion

## Lab Runtime Integration

- step_for_test() now:
  - Selects worker via RNG (worker_hint = rng % worker_count)
  - Attempts pop from assigned worker
  - Falls back to stealing from other workers
- Task completion calls scheduler.forget_task() for cleanup
- Uses record.complete() instead of direct state mutation

## Supporting Changes

- instrumented_future.rs: Minor updates for phase tracking integration
- oracle/determinism.rs: Compatibility updates
- virtual_time_wheel.rs: Minor formatting

The multi-worker model ensures:
- Same seed produces identical execution across runs
- Worker affinity is deterministic (round-robin assignment)
- Stealing decisions are deterministic (RNG-based)
- Cancel/timed priority is preserved per-worker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
